title: SQL Basics
description: Learn the foundational language of data. This course covers everything you need to start querying relational databases, filtering records, sorting results, and understanding database structures. Essential for any aspiring data professional.

seo_metadata.title: SQL Basics Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Start your journey in data analytics by learning SQL basics. Master SELECT statements, filtering with WHERE, and sorting with ORDER BY.

learning_outcomes: Understand the structure of relational databases
Write basic SELECT statements to retrieve data
Filter query results using the WHERE clause
Use aliases to improve query readability

modules.title: Introduction to Databases and SQL
modules.description: Understanding where data lives and how to ask for it.

lessons.title: What is a Relational Database?
lessons.content:
Database Concepts

A relational database organizes data into tables with columns and rows. Tables are linked together by relationships. SQL (Structured Query Language) is the standard language used to interact with them.

lessons.title: The SELECT Statement
lessons.content:
Retrieving Data

The SELECT statement is the foundation of SQL. You use it to specify which columns you want to view. Example: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM employees;

modules.title: Filtering Data
modules.description: How to retrieve only the specific records you need.

lessons.title: The WHERE Clause
lessons.content:
Conditional Retrieval

The WHERE clause filters rows based on a specified condition. Example: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE country = 'USA';

lessons.title: Logical Operators
lessons.content:
AND, OR, and NOT

Combine multiple conditions using logical operators. Example: WHERE country = 'USA' AND age > 25;

lessons.title: IN and BETWEEN
lessons.content:
Shorthand Filters

Use IN to check against a list of values, and BETWEEN to check within a range. Example: WHERE price BETWEEN 10 AND 50;

modules.title: Sorting and Formatting
modules.description: Organizing your output for easier reading.

lessons.title: The ORDER BY Clause
lessons.content:
Sorting Results

Use ORDER BY to sort your output. You can sort in ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC) order. Example: ORDER BY last_name ASC;

lessons.title: Column Aliases
lessons.content:
Renaming Columns

Use the AS keyword to give a column a temporary name in your output for better readability. Example: SELECT first_name AS "First Name" FROM employees;

mcqs.question: Which SQL statement is used to extract data from a database?
Which clause is used to filter records in SQL?
How do you select all columns from a table named 'Persons'?
Which operator is used to search for a specified pattern in a column?
Which keyword is used to sort the result-set?
How can you return all records from a table where the value of the column 'FirstName' is 'Peter'?

options.content: SELECT [all] FROM Persons WHERE FirstName LIKE 'Peter'

mcqs.question: Which operator selects values within a given range?
To sort results in descending order, you should use which keyword?
What is the purpose of the AS keyword in SQL?

options.content: To assign a temporary name (alias) to a column or table
